allies.rationale_text: Pareto-principle is the most accessible operational form of power-laws, turning skewed distributions into concrete prioritization moves.
allies.source_quote: captured most accessibly through the Pareto Principle

allies.rationale_text: Leverage-points helps power-laws focus on the few variables whose movement changes the whole system.
allies.source_quote: identify high-leverage points where a small change can produce a disproportionately large effect

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias can make power-laws lock onto a preferred 20 percent before the evidence justifies it.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Power-laws conflicts with base-rates when power-law distributions make average-based forecasts misleading because the mean is dominated by extreme values.
structured_tensions.source_quote: power-laws conflicts with base-rates (base rates) when power-law distributions make average-based forecasts misleading because the mean is dominated by extreme values.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Power-laws conflicts with simplification when Pareto shortcuts are applied to domains where value is more evenly distributed and the 80/20 heuristic does not hold.
structured_tensions.source_quote: power-laws conflicts with simplification (simplification) when Pareto shortcuts are applied to domains where value is more evenly distributed and the 80/20 heuristic does not hold.
